title: Paul Thomas Anderson Wins Golden Globe for Best Director, Tributes Late Collaborator
text: Paul Thomas Anderson claimed the Golden Globe for Best Director at Sunday's ceremony for his film 'One Battle After Another,' marking his second major directing win following his Critics Choice Award victory the previous week. He also won Best Screenplay at the Golden Globes. In his acceptance remarks, Anderson expressed gratitude for the audience's reception of his work. He delivered a special tribute to Warner Bros. chairperson Mike De Luca, crediting him with championing his vision for decades. Anderson stated: 'he came into my life and he wanted to be my champion and he single handedly supported me.' Most notably, Anderson dedicated the award to his late assistant director Adam Somner, who passed away from anaplastic thyroid cancer in 2024. He emphasized Somner's crucial contributions: 'If you get a good one like I had – the best one – what he did for me, for us, for this film, he is why I'm standing here.' The film also earned Teyana Taylor the Golden Globe for Performance by a Female Actor in a Supporting Role.
